title: fix: drop top_p when temperature is set for Bedrock Claude models
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Drops `top_p` in `parameterProcessor.ts` when `temperature` is also set for Bedrock Claude models (`*.anthropic.claude-*`) — Bedrock returns 400 when both are specified
- Extends the existing model-conditional param-strip pattern (Claude Opus 4.7/4.8 block)
- Adds test: temperature+top_p → top_p dropped; top_p alone → preserved

Fixes #9330

gist.headline: Claude plays nice with parameters
gist.blurb: Fixed a quirk where Bedrock Claude models would throw a fit if you sent both temperature and top_p. We now handle the parameter juggling for you behind the scenes.
gist.summary: The API now automatically drops the `top_p` parameter when `temperature` is also provided for Bedrock Claude models (`*.anthropic.claude-*`) to prevent 400 validation errors.
gist.impact: Developers can safely send both `temperature` and `top_p` in their requests to Claude models without encountering Bedrock API crashes.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee sorting parameters before they go into a machine. She keeps a thermometer (temperature) but tosses a block (top_p) into a bin to keep the machine running smoothly.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot carefully sorting glowing data blocks in front of a retro terminal. She is holding a tiny thermometer while tossing a block labeled 'top_p' into a lime green (#ecf874) recycling bin. Soft pastel gradient background with warm lighting. Chunky retro sprites, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
